outcome_contract.why: Prime inbox threads have no guest names, making triage slower for staff. Email threads already resolve names. Closing this gap makes the Prime inbox parity-complete for the most operator-visible field.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Prime direct (`prime_direct`) inbox threads display a booking-level guest name (lead guest or first named occupant from `guestsDetails/{bookingRef}`) in the inbox list view; broadcast and activity threads remain name-free; Firebase unavailability degrades gracefully to null names with no throw

engineering_coverage.applicable: Fail-open: error → null names, no UX change
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same fail-open; `Promise.allSettled` can handle partial failures per-ref
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B: slightly more precise partial-fail handling; Option A: all-or-nothing

engineering_coverage.applicable: PII in server memory only; no client exposure change
Log once: map build status + count + duration
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Log per-batch: how many refs fetched, how many found
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B allows finer-grained hit-rate logging

engineering_coverage.applicable: One mock for `fetchFirebaseJson` covering entire tree response
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: One mock per bookingRef call; `jest.fn()` chained resolves
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A test setup simpler; Option B tests are slightly more explicit

engineering_coverage.applicable: No type changes; `guestDetailsBookingPath` returns root path for empty string — guard mandatory
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same guard required; path called with validated ref only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Single large Firebase read; payload grows with historical bookings; timeout risk if tree is very large
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: N small reads; each bounded; easier to add timeout per-call
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B is more resilient to payload growth

engineering_coverage.applicable: Additive; no DB migration; rollback = revert augmentation call

stage_payload.decision_summary: The reception inbox permanently returns `guestFirstName: null` / `guestLastName: null` for all Prime messaging threads. The bookingId needed to look up the guest is already present on every `PrimeReviewThreadSummary`. The decision is which Firebase fetch strategy to use for the list-time augmentation — two options differ primarily on the single vs. batch lookup surface and how they handle the confirmed empty-string broadcast sentinel risk.
goals: Surface guest first and last name on `prime_direct` inbox list threads
Guard against `bookingId: ''` (broadcast) triggering a full-tree `guestsDetails/` read
Guard against `bookingId: 'activity'` (activity channel) triggering any Firebase call
Fail open: Firebase unavailable → names stay null, no throw, no visible error
non_goals: Augmenting detail/mutation paths (`getPrimeInboxThreadDetail`, `resolvePrimeInboxThread`, `dismissPrimeInboxThread`) — de-scoped to a follow-on
Changing how email thread guest names work
Occupant-level precision matching (booking-level lead guest is sufficient for inbox display)
constraints: `listPrimeInboxThreadSummaries` is async; augmentation can happen after the Prime API call, before return
assumptions: For `prime_direct` threads, `bookingId` is a valid Firebase `bookingRef`

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Up to 50 threads; each extra round-trip adds latency to inbox load
An unguarded empty bookingRef triggers a full `guestsDetails` tree read — correctness + performance risk
New code should match established `guest-matcher` / `firebase-rtdb` patterns
Change should be localized to `prime-review.server.ts` and new helper
New code paths must be verifiable in Jest without network calls

options_considered.Description: Fetch entire `guestsDetails/` root once, build in-memory `bookingRef → {firstName, lastName}` map, augment all summaries
options_considered.Upside: One Firebase call regardless of thread count; mirrors `buildGuestEmailMap` pattern exactly; simplest code
options_considered.Downside: Full tree read on every inbox list; payload grows as historical bookings accumulate
options_considered.Key risks: Historical booking accumulation may grow payload significantly over time

options_considered.Description: Collect unique non-sentinel bookingRefs from summaries, fire parallel `fetchFirebaseJson(guestDetailsBookingPath(ref))` calls
options_considered.Upside: Only fetches needed bookingRefs; payload bounded by current active guest count
options_considered.Downside: Multiple Firebase calls (up to N unique refs, typically 2-8 in practice); slightly more complex code
options_considered.Key risks: Marginally more code; `Promise.all` failure handling needed

options_considered.Description: Reuse the email-thread guest map (keyed by email) for Prime
options_considered.Downside: Email map is keyed by email; Prime threads have no guest email; not reusable without restructuring

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Add a new exported helper `fetchPrimeGuestNames(bookingRefs: string[])` in `apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/guest-matcher.server.ts`. Per unique valid bookingRef it fires two parallel `fetchFirebaseJson` calls — `bookings/{bookingRef}` (to find `leadGuest: true` occupantId) and `guestsDetails/{bookingRef}` (to read that occupant's name) — via `Promise.allSettled`. Empty/sentinel bookingRefs are filtered before any path is constructed. Returns `Map<string, { firstName: string; lastName: string }>`. Augment `listPrimeInboxThreadSummaries()` by calling this helper after mapping and patching the resu
chosen_approach.Concurrency cap: Fan-out is bounded: unique bookingRefs are deduplicated first; in practice 2–10 refs. Each ref triggers 2 Firebase calls (bookings + guestsDetails). A hard cap of 10 concurrent ref-pairs prevents pathological cases.
chosen_approach.Lead-guest strategy (dual-fetch): `guestsDetails/{bookingRef}/{occupantId}` does not carry a `leadGuest` flag — that flag lives in `bookings/{bookingRef}/{occupantId}.leadGuest`. Existing reception code (`usePrepaymentData.ts:252`, `useEmailProgressData.ts:195`) explicitly cross-references both paths. `fetchPrimeGuestNames` fetches both paths per unique ref, identifies the `leadGuest: true` occupantId from bookings, retrieves that occupant's name from guestsDetails. Falls back to first named occupant when no `leadGuest: true` is found.
chosen_approach.`mapPrimeSummaryToInboxThread` — NOT cha: Changing the mapper would widen blast radius to detail/resolve/dismiss paths (lines 436, 541, 562 in `prime-review.server.ts`) which are de-scoped. The broadcast `guestBookingRef: ''` pre-condition is left in place; the empty-string guard lives entirely in `fetchPrimeGuestNames` before any path construction.
chosen_approach.Option A (root fetch): Viable but rejected on payload-growth grounds. A single `guestsDetails/` root read grows with every historical booking. Under load (peak season) the tree could be large enough to cause timeout or slow the inbox list noticeably. Option B trades call count (2–8 vs 1) for bounded payload.
chosen_approach.Option C (sequential per-thread fetch): Rejected — N sequential round-trips at up to 50 threads is unacceptable latency.
chosen_approach.Option D (reuse email guest map): Rejected — the email map is email-keyed; Prime threads have no guest email at list time. Restructuring `buildGuestEmailMap` would widen blast radius unnecessarily.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (root fetch):** Viable but rejected on payload-growth grounds. A single `guestsDetails/` root read grows with every historical booking. Under load (peak season) the tree could be large enough to cause timeout or slow the inbox list noticeably. Option B trades call count (2–8 vs 1) for bounded payload.
**Option C (sequential per-thread fetch):** Rejected — N sequential round-trips at up to 50 threads is unacceptable latency.

**Option D (reuse email guest map):** Rejected — the email map is email-keyed; Prime threads have no guest email at list time. Restructuring `buildGuestEmailMap` would widen blast radius unnecessarily.

validation_implications: Tests run in CI only; `apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/__tests__/prime-review-mapper.test.ts` is the test file model (mocked `global.fetch`)
New tests for `listPrimeInboxThreadSummaries` must distinguish Prime API vs Firebase URL in `global.fetch` mock (e.g. `jest.fn().mockImplementation((url) => ...)`)
Existing `prime-review-mapper.test.ts` regression tests must still pass (mapper not changed)
sequencing_constraints: TASK-02 (helper `fetchPrimeGuestNames`) must precede TASK-03 (augmentation call in `listPrimeInboxThreadSummaries`) and TASK-05 (helper unit tests)
TASK-03 (augmentation) must precede TASK-07 (augmentation integration tests)
TASK-06 (API contract test updates) is independent; can run in parallel with TASK-02
risks_to_carry_forward: Dual-endpoint `global.fetch` mock setup is moderately complex; if done wrong tests pass against wrong mock data — reviewer must verify mock URL routing explicitly

areas.label: Prime thread list guest names
areas.current: `listPrimeInboxThreadSummaries()` returns all threads with `guestFirstName: null`, `guestLastName: null`
areas.future: (1) Prime API call returns `PrimeReviewThreadSummary[]`; (2) `summaries.map(mapPrimeSummaryToInboxThread)` builds base `InboxThreadSummaryApiModel[]` with null names; (3) unique non-sentinel bookingRefs extracted; (4) `fetchPrimeGuestNames(refs)` fires parallel Firebase reads; (5) names patched into summaries before return
areas.steady: Email thread path unchanged; `mapPrimeSummaryToInboxThread` stays synchronous; detail/mutation paths remain null (de-scoped)
areas.seams: Firebase unavailability must degrade gracefully — tested in build

areas.label: Sentinel guard (`'activity'` + empty string)
areas.current: `guestBookingRef` already nullified for `prime_activity`; broadcast threads emit `guestBookingRef: ''` (existing mapper pre-condition; NOT changed)
areas.trigger: Any inbox list request including broadcast threads
areas.future: `fetchPrimeGuestNames` filters empty/sentinel bookingRefs before constructing any Firebase path; `prime_activity` ('activity') and broadcast ('') bookingRefs never reach `bookingRootPath` or `guestDetailsBookingPath`
areas.steady: `mapPrimeSummaryToInboxThread` unchanged; broadcast `guestBookingRef: ''` unchanged; guard is internal to helper only
areas.seams: Empty-string guard must be tested explicitly to prevent root-fetch regression

areas.label: UI display
areas.current: `ThreadList.tsx` renders name when non-null; shows nothing when null
areas.future: Guest name appears in Prime direct thread list rows
areas.steady: All other thread list fields unchanged
